subject: Geography
chapter: Geographical Perspective on Selected Issues and Problems

remember: Identify the geographical perspective on selected issues and problems.
List and explain the different approaches to geographical perspective.
Recall the importance and relevance of geographical perspective in understanding contemporary issues and problems.
understand: Explain the concept of geographical perspective and its significance in understanding human and physical geography.
Describe the role of geographical perspective in framing policies and strategies for sustainable development.
Understand the relationship between geographical perspective and environmental degradation.
apply: Apply geographical perspective to analyze and understand contemporary issues and problems such as climate change, urbanization, and sustainable development.
Use geographical perspective to evaluate the effectiveness of policies and strategies in addressing environmental and social issues.
Apply geographical perspective to identify and recommend solutions to environmental and social problems.
analyze: Analyze the relationship between geographical perspective and human well-being.
Examine the impact of geographical perspective on decision-making and policy formulation.
Analyze the role of geographical perspective in promoting sustainable development and reducing environmental degradation.

ncert_objectives: To understand the concept of geographical perspective and its importance in understanding contemporary issues and problems.
To analyze the relationship between geographical perspective and human well-being, environmental degradation, and sustainable development.
To apply geographical perspective to identify and recommend solutions to environmental and social problems.
board_exam_objectives: Students will be able to explain the concept of geographical perspective and its significance in understanding human and physical geography.
Students will be able to analyze and evaluate the effectiveness of policies and strategies in addressing environmental and social issues using geographical perspective.
Students will be able to apply geographical perspective to identify and recommend solutions to environmental and social problems.
